texto: Start with the observation that American law is not designed to catch and punish every instance of illegal conduct. Nothing short of a totalitarian state could do that. It is designed to prosecute persons when there is a reasonable basis for believing they have committed a crime, and then to convict them when there is no reasonable doubt that they are guilty.
explicacao: Citação de Dale Carpenter sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Start with the observation that American law is not designed to catch and punish every instance o…” articula reflexao, 2012, jurisprudence em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como jurisprudence e law.
